title: podmansh.1.md: `UID=$(id -u lockedu)` fails with `-bash: UID: readonly variable`
body:
### Issue Description

The command `UID=$(id -u lockedu)` suggested in the [podmansh](https://docs.podman.io/en/v4.6.1/markdown/podmansh.1.html)  man page version 4.6.1 fails



```
[root@fedora38 ~]# UID=$(id -u lockedu)
-bash: UID: readonly variable
[root@fedore38 ~]#
```



Markdown source code:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/9cd42869223ab8acdd5be3d9bb2a84d988d8d73f/docs/source/markdown/podmansh.1.md?plain=1#L37

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  `ssh root@fedora38`
2.  `UID=$(id -u lockedu)`



### Describe the results you received

Step 2 fails with the error message

```
-bash: UID: readonly variable
```

### Describe the results you expected

No failure

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

The shell used in the reproducer  is `/bin/bash`

The user __lockedu__ exists.

```
[root@fedora38 ~]# id -u lockedu
50191
[root@fedora38 ~]#
```

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting
